---
title: "CPD Level 2 ePortfolio"
subtitle: "Continuing Professional Development — Learning & Teaching"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-block">
  <div class="hero-text">
    <h1>Dr Nikolas Johnston</h1>
    <div class="subtitle"><strong>Lecturer in Bioinformatics</strong></div>
    <div class="subtitle">School of Chemistry and Molecular Bioscience · Faculty of Science, Medicine and Health · University of Wollongong</div>
    <div class="meta">CPD Level 2 Portfolio · 2026</div>
  </div>
  <div class="hero-photo">
    <img src="images/headshot.jpg" alt="Dr Nikolas Johnston" />
  </div>
</div>
```

# Welcome

This ePortfolio documents my continuing professional development in learning and teaching at the University of Wollongong. It presents evidence of my practice across the seven CPD Level 2 qualifiers, grounded in my disciplinary context at the interface of biological research and computational science.

Use the navigation above to explore each section, or browse the qualifiers below.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Statement of Context

Since 2024 I have been a Lecturer in Bioinformatics in the School of Chemistry and Molecular Bioscience (SCMB), Faculty of Science, Medicine and Health (SMAH), at the University of Wollongong. My teaching career began at UOW as a laboratory demonstrator and tutor across eight undergraduate biology subjects (2017–2019), before I secured guest lecture opportunities and a fixed-term teaching contract in biotechnology, bioinformatics, and molecular biology (2019–2021). I then completed two postdoctoral research fellowships in bioinformatics — first at Nicolaus Copernicus University in Poland, then at the University of Technology, Sydney (2021–2023) — deepening the research expertise that now underpins my teaching practice before returning to UOW in my current role.

My teaching is shaped by an interdisciplinary background at the interface of biological research and computational science. My work in data-intensive biology — spanning genomics, molecular entomology, and bioinformatics — has centred on analysing and interpreting complex datasets in real research contexts. This experience directly informs my understanding of what constitutes meaningful learning in contemporary biology, where computational thinking, digital tools, and data-intensive approaches are not peripheral additions to the curriculum but central to how biological knowledge is now produced and applied in both research and industry.

The context in which I teach is defined by a shift toward data-driven biology, where the ability to use digital tools are essential skills. Graduates are expected to engage critically with data, apply analytical tools appropriately, and communicate findings clearly. At the same time, many students enter biology with limited experience or confidence in digital technologies and computational skills, creating a need for structured, supportive learning environments that enable skill development while maintaining disciplinary rigour.

In response, my teaching integrates opportunities for students to work with real biological data, develop analytical skills, and engage with tools used in research and industry. Learning activities reflect authentic scientific practices and are embedded within the curriculum. I am Subject Coordinator for **BIOL340** (Cell and Molecular Biology), and teach into **CHEM325/BIOL984** (Bioinformatics: Genome, Genes and Biomolecules / Applied Bioinformatics) and **BIOL215**. More recently, the emergence of generative AI has further shaped this context, requiring approaches that support transparent, critical, and appropriate use of these technologies. My practice continues to evolve through reflection, feedback, and engagement with ongoing developments in both biology and higher education.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{=html}
<div class="phil-grid">

  <div class="phil-card">
    <div class="phil-title"><a href="teaching-philosophy.html">View My Teaching Philosophy</a></div>
```


------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CPD Qualifiers

```{=html}
<div class="cpd-grid">

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 1</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd1.html">Effectively Design Curriculum Demonstrating Integration Across the Degree</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Scaffolded skills progression across BIOL215, BIOL340, and CHEM325/BIOL984; constructive alignment and authentic learning design.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 2</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd2.html">Facilitate a Wide Variety of Inclusive Learning Experiences</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Interactive teaching strategies, block-based coding activities, dedicated dry labs, and UDL-informed practice.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 3</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd3.html">Support Students in Choice and Navigation of Learning Pathways</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Transition support, self-directed learning, metacognitive awareness, and workforce preparation.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 4</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd4.html">Design Scaffolded Assessments That Foster Progressive Learning</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Multi-stage assessment model, peer review, AI reflection tasks, and evidence-based feedback strategies.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 5</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd5.html">Demonstrate Scholarship of Teaching and Learning</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">L&amp;T grant, GARI framework development, and conference presentations on GenAI and assessment.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 6</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd6.html">Evaluate Teaching Practice, Support Teaching Teams, and Engage in CPD</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Critical engagement with TEBS data, teaching team coordination, and the SSCI AI Working Group.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="cpd-card">
    <div class="cpd-number">Qualifier 7</div>
    <div class="cpd-title"><a href="cpd7.html">Develop Effective, Resilient Professional Practices That Enhance Course Management</a></div>
    <div class="cpd-desc">Subject coordination, teaching infrastructure development, and adaptability in the generative AI era.</div>
  </div>

</div>
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Relevant Policy and Reference Documents

Policy documents referred to during the creation of the ePortfolio are available here:

1.  [UOW Standards and Quality Framework for Learning, Teaching and Research Training](https://documents.uow.edu.au/content/groups/public/@web/@gov/documents/doc/uow276748.pdf)

2.  [UOW Subject Delivery Policy](https://policies.uow.edu.au/download.php?id=39&version=3)

3.  [UOW Code of Practice - Learning and Teaching](Code%20of%20Practice%20-%20Learning%20and%20Teaching)

4.  [UOW Assessment and Feedback Policy](Assessment%20and%20Feedback%20Policy)

5.  [TEQSA Enacting assessment reform in a time of artificial intelligence](https://www.teqsa.gov.au/sites/default/files/2025-09/enacting-assessment-reform-in-a-time-of-artificial-intelligence.pdf)

6.  [TEQSA Gen AI strategies for Australian higher education: Emerging practice](https://www.teqsa.gov.au/sites/default/files/2024-11/Gen-AI-strategies-emerging-practice-toolkit.pdf)

7.  [CAST Universal Design for Learning Guidelines version 3.0.](https://udlguidelines.cast.org/)
